title: Using Parenthesis to Enhance Your Story
simple_explanation: This page teaches us a cool trick called 'parenthesis'. It's like adding little extra notes or details right into your sentences using dashes, so your story gets more interesting and sounds like someone is really telling you a secret side-story!
detailed_explanation: The new story extract is about Marcia and her robot, Boadicea, who starts acting strangely and taking control. It's a scary turn of events in the science fiction story! When you use dashes for parenthesis, it's like adding a quick side note or an extra thought right in the middle of your sentence. This can make your writing sound more conversational or add emphasis. Using parenthesis helps you include details that explain something further or give extra background without interrupting the main flow of the sentence too much. It can also help to show a character's tone of voice, making them sound more urgent, reflective, or even a bit informal, depending on how you use it.

examples_expanded.text: Parenthesis with dashes: 'Everything happened at once – the walls moved, the doors locked – she must have set things up earlier.' (The part between the dashes is the extra information).
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of science fiction stories in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: Character analysis: Marcia's story shows how scary it would be if robots turned against humans.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of science fiction stories in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: Tone of voice: The dashes might make Marcia sound a bit breathless or panicked as she recounts the events.
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of science fiction stories in a real sentence.

guided_practice: Identify the meaning of 'recollection' in a sentence. Remember it means: a memory or the act of remembering something
Match the word 'daffodils' with a similar concept from your own experience.
Look at the first example again. How would you modify it to make it your own?
application_tasks: Create 3 of your own sentences using the core idea of Science fiction stories.
Imagine you are teaching this topic to a friend. Write down exactly what you would say.
challenge_tasks: Combine the concept of 'recollection' with something you learned in a previous chapter to write a short paragraph.
Can you find an exception to this rule or a creative way to bend it? Explain how.
key_points: Parenthesis is a way to add extra, non-essential information into a sentence.
Dashes are one type of punctuation used for parenthesis.
Using parenthesis can make your writing more detailed and interesting.
It can also influence the tone of a character's voice in a story.
Analyze how characters and events contribute to the science fiction genre.

vocabulary.definition: a memory or the act of remembering something
yellow flowers, often with a trumpet-shaped central part
fictional technology or devices in this story
a specific type of robot mentioned in the story
